response:
[IDENTIFY CORE TASK] THE MAIN OBJECTIVE IS TO CALCULATE THE DIFFERENCE IN TIME PER QUESTION BETWEEN CHEMISTRY AND BIOLOGY TESTS.

[DEFINE ROLE] SOPHIA HAS TO ALLOCATE EQUAL TIME PER QUESTION FOR EACH TEST.

[SET CONSTRAINTS] SHE HAS 45 MINUTES FOR BIOLOGY AND 60 MINUTES FOR CHEMISTRY.

[OUTPUT FORMAT] CALCULATE THE DIFFERENCE IN SECONDS PER QUESTION BETWEEN THE TWO TESTS.

[ERROR PREVENTION] MAKE SURE TO CONVERT MINUTES TO SECONDS AND CALCULATE THE DIFFERENCE ACCURATELY.

[FINAL ANSWER] [CALCULATE TIME PER QUESTION FOR BIOLOGY] 45 MINUTES = 2700 SECONDS, 2700 / 25 = 108 SECONDS PER QUESTION. [CALCULATE TIME PER QUESTION FOR CHEMISTRY] 60 MINUTES = 3600 SECONDS, 3600 / 20 = 180 SECONDS PER QUESTION. [DIFFERENCE] 180 - 108 = 72 SECONDS. [FINAL ANSWER] 72.
